questions.question: How long does your compression cycle typically take?
questions.description: Time from final numbers to compensation reporting

questions.question: What percentage of your comp process is manual?
questions.description: Includes spreadsheets, email, manual data entry, reconciliation

questions.question: How easily can you forecast impact of comp changes?
How confident are you in your data quality?
questions.description: Accuracy and completeness of territories, quotas, and performance data

questions.question: What's slowing you down? (Select all that apply)

gapTypes.name: Compression Cycle Gap
gapTypes.description: Your compensation cycle takes longer than it should
gapTypes.impact: Sales teams wait weeks for comp clarity. Finance has too many manual touchpoints.
gapTypes.solution: Compress cycles from 8 weeks to 3 weeks with automation and streamlined workflows
metrics: 3 weeks average compression (vs. 8 weeks industry average)

gapTypes.name: Manual Process Gap
gapTypes.description: Too much manual work is slowing your team down
gapTypes.impact: Finance team spends 60% of time on data entry and reconciliation instead of strategy.
gapTypes.solution: Automate manual processes with data integration and validation rules

gapTypes.name: Visibility & Forecasting Gap
gapTypes.description: You lack real-time visibility into compensation impact
gapTypes.impact: Business changes take weeks to model. You can't quickly answer what-if questions.
gapTypes.solution: Real-time dashboards and forecasting models for instant business scenario modeling
metrics: What-if scenarios run in minutes vs. days

gapTypes.name: Data Quality Gap
gapTypes.description: Data silos and quality issues are limiting optimization
gapTypes.impact: Territory, quota, and performance data are fragmented. Decisions based on incomplete info.
gapTypes.solution: Unified data layer integrating territories, quotas, performance, and comp
metrics: Single source of truth for all SPM data
